packages.purpose: SQL-on-files and reproducible intermediate tables for large simulation runs.
Fast columnar data transforms for scenario grids and evidence joins.
Local schema validation for LLM outputs and handoff artifacts.
Typed runtime models for simulation objects and API envelopes.
Vector geospatial dataframes for boundaries, POI layers, and spatial joins.
Geometry operations for boundaries, buffers, distances, and validity checks.
Graph representation for routes, entrances, and walkable network prototypes.
Street-network acquisition and routing prototypes; not a substitute for Amap/DWG validation.
Trajectory objects and movement-analysis vocabulary for later real track calibration.
Python agent-based modeling prototype layer for personas and behavior programs.
Geospatial ABM extension; candidate bridge between agents and map geometries.
Discrete-event queues for service, waiting, stockout, and operating-hour constraints.
Sensitivity analysis for deciding which uncertain inputs matter most.
Parameter search and calibration once field observations or reliable proxies exist.

intentionally_deferred.name: Ray
intentionally_deferred.reason: Useful for very large distributed agent execution, but heavy for current local P2/P3 validation.

intentionally_deferred.name: MATSim / SUMO
intentionally_deferred.reason: Good external engines for mature transport simulation, but require clean road network, demand, and calibration data first.

intentionally_deferred.name: AnyLogic / Unreal
intentionally_deferred.reason: Can support high-fidelity demos later, but would distract from evidence, schema, and calibration gates now.

architecture_note: Modern local stack is ready for evidence-bound hybrid simulation: domain generator, geospatial constraints, agent/event prototypes, sensitivity/calibration, and strict LLM contracts.
